mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate labor income, which directly supports consumption expenditures and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Median S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative following month-over-month PAYEMS increases over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at or below 0.25% signals extreme monetary accommodation that suppresses the equity discount rate and boosts relative risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) deliver a stagflationary shock that simultaneously raises input cost pressures and discount rates, compressing profit margins and valuation multiples to produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: rising inflation with tight monetary policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive median 20-day returns during months when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS increase month-over-month over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment environment, a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market healing and falling recession risk, triggering portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0% and declines month-over-month, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of instances over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) falling below its trailing 12-month moving average indicates a consumer demand shortfall that lowers corporate revenue expectations and triggers near-term equity derating, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when RSAFS is below its 12-month moving average over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) alongside falling housing starts (HOUST) signals a supply-demand disconnect where goods production outpaces underlying end demand, leading to inventory accumulation and negative earnings surprises that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where INDPRO rises month-over-month and HOUST falls month-over-month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms aggregate demand expansion and reduces recession probability, supporting positive medium-term equity risk premia and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth coincides with negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.5% triggers investor fears of collapsing consumer demand and rising credit defaults, driving institutional de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of weeks when UNRATE exceeds 6.5% over a 3-year rolling window.
